question: Australia has announced the return of 3 ancient Indian antiquities of which dynasty, stolen from temples in Tamil Nadu?
detail:
- **Australia** announced the return of **3 ancient Indian antiquities** taken illegally abroad from temples in **Tamil Nadu**.
- The announcement was made during Prime Minister **Narendra Modi's** visit to Australia for the **India-Australia Annual Summit**. The return will happen under the **Mutual Legal Assistance Treaty (MLAT)**.
- **The 3 antiquities:** the **Bronze Trident of Goddess Bhadrakali**, a **Granite Nandi Idol**, and a **Six-headed Karthikeya (Shanmukha) sculpture**.
- All three are mainly from the **11th–12th century**, the period of the powerful **Chola dynasty**, famous for its outstanding **bronze sculpture** and grand temple architecture.
- **About the Chola Dynasty (c. 850–1279 CE):** a powerful South Indian dynasty especially known for temple architecture and bronze sculpture. Region — **Tamil Nadu**; capital — **Thanjavur** (later **Gangaikonda Cholapuram**); famous king — **Rajendra Chola I**.

question: Which Indian tennis player has been appointed as the Player Ambassador of the Asian Tennis Federation (ATF)?
detail:
- Indian tennis player **Leander Paes** has been appointed **Player Ambassador** of the **Asian Tennis Federation (ATF)**.
- In this role he will work to popularise tennis across Asia, inspire young players and support the ATF's development programmes.
- His aim is to promote **grassroots tennis**, encourage youngsters to take up the game, and grow the sport across Asia.
- **About Leander Paes:** from **Kolkata, West Bengal**; one of India's most successful tennis players. Olympic medal — **Bronze (Atlanta Olympics, 1996)**; **18 Grand Slam titles** (8 Men's Doubles + 10 Mixed Doubles). In **2024** he became the **first Indian** inducted into the **International Tennis Hall of Fame**.
- **Awards:** Arjuna Award (**1990**), Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna (**1996–97**, now Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna), Padma Shri (**2001**), Padma Bhushan (**2014**).
- **About the ATF:** the highest governing body for tennis in Asia. Established **1956**; headquarters **Colombo, Sri Lanka**.

question: What is the name of the world's first once-weekly basal insulin launched in India?
detail:
- India launched the world's first **once-weekly basal insulin, Awiqli**, developed by the Danish pharmaceutical company **Novo Nordisk**.
- Awiqli uses **Insulin Icodec** as its active ingredient and is approved for adults with **Type 1 and Type 2 diabetes**.
- Its biggest feature: it is taken as an injection **once a week** instead of every day — **52 injections a year instead of 365**.
- **What is basal insulin:** the body needs small amounts of insulin around the clock. The insulin that works continuously through the day and night is called **basal insulin**. It is a **long-acting** insulin that keeps blood glucose controlled between meals and during sleep. Also known as **background insulin**.
- **About Novo Nordisk:** from **Denmark**; established **1923**; works on diabetes care, obesity and rare disease medicines.
- **About insulin:** a hormone made by the **beta cells of the pancreas**. Discovered by **Frederick Banting & Charles Best (1921)**. **World Diabetes Day — 14 November**, on Sir Frederick Banting's birthday.

question: Where was the 'International Centre for Civilizational Studies – Manas and Mahabharata' inaugurated?
detail:
- The **International Centre for Civilizational Studies – 'Manas and Mahabharata'** was inaugurated in **Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan**, with the cooperation of India and Kyrgyzstan.
- It is a **research and cultural centre** where study and research will be done on great epics like India's **Mahabharata** and Kyrgyzstan's **Manas**.
- It has been set up jointly by the **Manas National Academy (Kyrgyzstan)** and the **Centre for Studies of International Relations (CSIR), New Delhi**.
- Its aim is to promote civilizational studies, cultural exchange and intercultural dialogue through these great epics.
- On the occasion, the **first Hindi translation** of the Kyrgyz epic **'Manas'** was also released.
- **Manas epic:** Kyrgyzstan's **national epic**, considered one of the **longest oral epics** in the world. It describes the life and courage of the warrior Manas and the history of the Kyrgyz people.
- **Mahabharata:** a **Sanskrit epic**, India's most famous ancient epic, composed by **Maharishi Ved Vyasa**.
- **Kyrgyzstan:** capital **Bishkek**.

question: Who has become India's 98th Chess Grandmaster?
detail:
- **S. Aswath**, a 17-year-old chess player from **Tamil Nadu**, became India's **98th Grandmaster (GM)**.
- He achieved it by earning his **third and final GM norm** at the **Pune International GM Tournament**.
- He had earlier earned two GM norms at the **Grenke Open 2025** and the **First Saturday GM Tournament (Budapest)**, and had already crossed a **2500+ FIDE rating**.
- S. Aswath is Tamil Nadu's **38th Grandmaster**.
- **About FIDE (Fédération Internationale des Échecs):** the governing body of world chess — it makes the rules and runs the World Championships and international ratings (**Elo rating**). Established **1924**; headquarters **Lausanne, Switzerland**.
- **Note:** the **97th GM** was **G. B. Harshavardhan**, also from Tamil Nadu, in **June 2026**.

question: Where will ICMR establish India's first High Altitude Medicine & Public Health Research Centre?
detail:
- The **Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR)** will set up India's first **High Altitude Medicine & Public Health Research Centre** at **Keylong (Lahaul-Spiti), Himachal Pradesh**.
- The centre will research the health of people living in high Himalayan regions, **high altitude diseases**, climate-sensitive diseases and **mountain medicine**.
- It will also use modern technologies like **telemedicine, digital health, drone-based medicine delivery** and real-time disease surveillance.
- **About ICMR:** India's largest medical research institution, working on disease prevention, treatment and health research. Established **1911** (as the Indian Research Fund Association); renamed **ICMR** in **1949**; headquarters **New Delhi**; under the **Ministry of Health & Family Welfare**.

question: Where has the Geological Survey of India (GSI) discovered thorium deposits?
detail:
- The **Geological Survey of India (GSI)** has discovered deposits of the thorium-bearing mineral **Monazite** in the **Chamarajanagar district of Karnataka** (in which Gundlupet lies).
- The deposits have been identified over roughly **700 acres**, of which **20 acres** have been declared a **core area** and are being prepared for mining.
- **About thorium:** a naturally occurring **radioactive metal**, seen as important as a clean nuclear fuel of the future. It cannot be used directly as fuel in a nuclear reactor — it first has to be converted into **Uranium-233 (U-233)**. **Atomic number — 90**.
- **Monazite** is a rare mineral containing mainly thorium, **rare earth elements (REEs)** and a little uranium.
- **About GSI:** a Government of India organisation that carries out geological surveys and the search for minerals, rocks and natural resources. Established **1851**; headquarters **Kolkata**; under the **Ministry of Mines**.

question: The 'Samriddh Gram' project that won the Global Winner Award at the WSIS (World Summit on the Information Society) Prizes 2026 has been developed by which ministry?
detail:
- The **Department of Telecommunications (DoT)**'s **Samriddh Gram – Integrated Phygital Service Delivery Model** won the **Global Winner Award** in the **'Enabling Environment'** category at the **WSIS Prizes 2026**.
- The project delivers services like healthcare, education, agriculture, financial inclusion, skilling and e-governance to rural areas through **BharatNet**, via **Samriddhi Kendras**.
- **What is Samriddh Gram:** a Government of India **digital village initiative** meant to make government and digital services easily available to villagers, through a **phygital** (physical + digital) model. It provides telemedicine, education, agriculture, e-governance, digital banking and skill development.
- **About the WSIS Prizes:** organised and awarded by the **International Telecommunication Union (ITU)** under the United Nations' **WSIS (World Summit on the Information Society)** process. ITU headquarters — **Geneva, Switzerland**.

question: Which institution has developed India's first indigenous Green Refrigerant Technology (HFO-1234yf)?
options: CSIR – Central Institute of Mining and Fuel Research
CSIR – Indian Institute of Chemical Technology
detail:
- **CSIR–Indian Institute of Chemical Technology (CSIR-IICT)**, Hyderabad, has developed India's first indigenous **Green Refrigerant Technology (HFO-1234yf)**.
- It is an environment-friendly alternative to the **hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs)** used in refrigeration and air conditioning.
- The technology is ready for **transfer to industry** and will boost **Atmanirbhar Bharat**.
- **About HFO-1234yf:** full form **Hydrofluoroolefin-1234yf**; a green refrigerant used in AC and refrigeration systems, replacing HFCs. It will be used to produce cooling in air conditioners, refrigerators and car ACs.
- **About CSIR-IICT:** one of India's major chemical research institutes, researching new medicines, chemicals, eco-friendly technologies and industrial processes. Location — **Hyderabad, Telangana**; under the **Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR)**.

question: Which country will host the Global AI Summit 2027?
detail:
- **Switzerland** has announced that it will host the **Global AI Summit 2027**.
- The summit will be held on **21–22 June 2027** in **Geneva**.
- Its aim is to promote **AI governance, trustworthy AI, responsible AI** and international cooperation. Representatives of governments, international organisations, scientists, industry and civil society will take part.
- **About the Global AI Summit:** an international conference meant to promote global cooperation and policy-making for the safe, responsible and human-centred use of Artificial Intelligence.
- **Editions:** **2023** — Bletchley Park (United Kingdom); **2026** — New Delhi (India), held as the **India AI Impact Summit** by the **IndiaAI Mission** under **MeitY**; **2027** — Geneva (Switzerland); **2028** — United Arab Emirates.
